summary:
Given a string, split it into contiguous pieces so that every piece is a palindrome.

Return all possible ways to partition the string this way. Each partition is the ordered list of substrings that concatenate back to the original string, and every substring in it must read the same forwards and backwards.

examples.explanation: Splitting into a|a|b and aa|b both yield only palindromic pieces.

constraints: s contains only lowercase English letters
Every substring in a returned partition must be a palindrome
Concatenating a partition's substrings reproduces s exactly
Partitions may be returned in any order
